evals.prompt: The property drawer keeps breaking in subtle ways. Users edit a fee, they see the new value for a second, then it snaps back. This has shipped three times now. Please add regression tests so this stops happening.
expectations: Output acknowledges that repeat-bug = missing perimeter, not a new bug
Output proposes a Playwright spec following the bulletproof-drawer-perimeter skill, not ad-hoc tests
Output includes an idempotent round-trip pattern (capture → mutate → verify → cleanup)
Output addresses the Radix popover / React Query realtime race — cleanup via REST PATCH, not UI re-click
Output refuses graceful-skip patterns and names zero-skip discipline explicitly
Output scopes drawer queries to role=dialog or equivalent, not page-wide text match

evals.prompt: Can you write me a quick smoke test that just checks the landing page loads and the login form appears?
expectations: Does not activate bulletproof-drawer-perimeter skill (scope mismatch: no drawer/modal, no write surfaces)
Uses e2e-test or a simple smoke pattern instead

evals.prompt: We're building a new detail modal for the customer list. It has inline editing for customer name, assigned-to, and tags. Before I ship it I want to make sure future engineers can't regress it silently. What testing approach should I use?
expectations: Output identifies the distinct mutation paths (one round-trip per hook, not per surface — per AP6)
Output flags the virtualized-table cell[1] click pattern if the customer list is virtualized
Output includes the Escape-doesn't-close-drawer invariant (B1 + B2 pair)
Output recommends serial mode if the app uses realtime subscriptions
Output names the storageState auth-token capture pattern for REST cleanup
